zh.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares於1359年出生在英國越可爾。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1381年在他的家鄉享年22歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares于1359年出生在英国越可爾。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1381年在他的家乡享年22岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mares
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Luis, Mario, Ruben, Arturo, Guadalupe, Rafael, Ramon.
Catalan (Marès, also Marés): topographic name from Catalan marès ‘by the sea’.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Le Marais-la-Chapelle (Calvados), Marais-Vernier (Eure), or various other minor places in northern France named with Old French marais, Norman-Picard marese ‘marsh’.

variants.name: Maris
meanings: English: variant of   Mares   2.
Flemish and Dutch: metronymic from the personal name Marie (see Maria ).
Dutch (Van Maris): habitational name for someone from Maris in Dutch Limburg.

variants.name: Marschke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Otto.

German (Silesia, Saxony, and Bohemia): from a Germanized Slavic pet form of the Old Slavic personal name Maroslav, with the first element probably based on the root mar- ‘to ruin’, or of a personal name such as Czech and Slovak Mareš (see Mares 4).

variants.name: Marez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Lupe, Jesus, Armando, Juan, Juanita, Leandro, Ruben, Adelfa, Alfredo, Amalia, Ana.
Hispanic: altered form of Catalan Marès (see Mares ), influenced by the Spanish patronymic suffix -ez.
French (Nord): variant of   Maret  .

variants.name: Merris
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mares   2.

variants.name: Maria
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Elena, Manuel, Pedro, Juan, Miguel, Rafael, Santa, Alejandro, Amado, Amandio.  Portuguese Joaquim.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Salvatore, Albertina, Antonino, Cataldo, Filiberto, Ignazio, Plinio, Sal.

Spanish (María); Italian and Portuguese; Hungarian (Mária): from the female personal name, Latin Maria. This was the name of the mother of Jesus Christ in the New Testament, as well as several other New Testament figures. The Latin form of the name, on which all common variants from European languages are based (including English Mary ), derived as a back-formation from the early Christian female name Mariam. This was mistaken for an accusative case, with the usual Latin feminine accusative ending -am, but in fact it is an Aramaic form (originally Maryam) of the Hebrew name Miryam. The Hebrew name is of uncertain etymology, perhaps from a word meaning ‘wished-for child’, from an Egyptian root mrj with the addition of the Hebrew feminine diminutive suffix -am. Saint Jerome understood it as a compound of mar ‘drop’ + yam ‘sea’, which he rendered as Latin stilla maris, later altered to stella maris ‘star of the sea’, whence the medieval Christian liturgical phrase.
French: Latinized form of   Marie  , a cognate of 1 above. It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin.

variants.name: Maresh
meanings: Americanized form of Czech and Slovak Mareš (see Mares ).

variants.name: Marris
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mares   2.
Dutch: variant of   Maris   2.
Americanized form of Greek   Maris   4 or 5.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares was born in 1359, in Yorkshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1381, in his hometown, at the age of 22.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares est né en 1359, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1381, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 22 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares wurde 1359 in Yorkshire, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1381 im Alter von 22 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares è nato il nel 1359 nel Yorkshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1381, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 22 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Maresは1359年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1381年に故郷で22歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares(은)는 1359년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1381년 그의 고향에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares nasceu em 1359, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1381, em sua cidade natal, com 22 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares родился в 1359 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1381 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 22 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas de Mares nació en 1359, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1381, en su pueblo, a la edad de 22 años.
